title: Chester's Way
description: A story about Chester and Wilson, two best friends who share the exact same habits, and how their world expands when they meet a unique girl named Lilly.

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester had his own way of doing things.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特有他自己做事的一套方式。

phraseNotes.title: have one's own way of doing things
phraseNotes.explanation: 指某人有自己獨特或固定的行事風格及習慣。

examples.english: My grandfather has his own way of doing things in the garden.
examples.chinese: 我爺爺在整理花園時有他自己的一套方式。

sentences.englishOriginal: He always cut his sandwiches diagonally.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他總是把三明治對角切開。

vocabularyNotes.title: diagonally
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 副詞，意為「對角地」或「斜對角地」。

examples.english: Fold the square piece of paper diagonally.
examples.chinese: 將這張正方形的紙沿對角線折起來。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he never left the house without double-knotting his shoes.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且他出門前一定會把鞋帶綁兩道結。

grammarNotes.title: never... without...
grammarNotes.explanation: 雙重否定句型，用來強調「每逢……必定……」或「做某事時一定要……」。

examples.english: She never leaves the house without her umbrella.

nativeExpressionNotes.title: double-knotting his shoes
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 綁雙重結以防止鞋帶鬆開，是英文描述綁鞋帶時非常道地寫實的說法。

examples.english: Make sure you double-knot your shoes before the race.
examples.chinese: 跑步比賽前記得要把鞋帶綁緊、打個雙重結。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he always carried a miniature first-aid kit in his back pocket. Just in case.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且他總是在後口袋裡放一個微型急救包。以防萬一。

vocabularyNotes.title: miniature
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意為「微型的」、「袖珍的」或「小型的」。

examples.english: He collects miniature cars as a hobby.
examples.chinese: 他的愛好是收集模型微型汽車。

phraseNotes.title: just in case
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見的固定片語，意為「以防萬一」或「以備不時之需」。

examples.english: Take an extra key, just in case.
examples.chinese: 多帶一把鑰匙吧，以防萬一。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester's best friend Wilson was exactly the same way.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特最好的朋友威爾遜也完全是一模一樣。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: exactly the same way
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 用來形容某人的習慣、作風或反應與另一個人「完全一樣」，語氣十分道地自然。

examples.english: My brother behaves exactly the same way when he is nervous.
examples.chinese: 我弟弟緊張時的反應完全是一模一樣。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester wouldn't play baseball unless Wilson played, and they never swung at the first pitch or slid headfirst.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 除非威爾遜也上場，否則切斯特就不打棒球，而且他們絕不在第一球就揮棒，也從不頭朝前滑壘。

phraseNotes.title: swing at the first pitch
phraseNotes.explanation: 棒球術語，指「對第一球揮棒」，在這裡也暗示做決定時非常謹慎、不輕舉妄動。

examples.english: The coach told the batter not to swing at the first pitch.
examples.chinese: 教練告訴打者不要對第一球挥棒。

grammarNotes.title: wouldn't... unless...
grammarNotes.explanation: 表示「除非……否則不……」，用來表達某種強烈的條件或堅持。

examples.english: He wouldn't go to the party unless his friend came along.
examples.chinese: 除非他朋友一起去，否則他是不會去參加派對的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Some days I can't tell those two apart, said Wilson's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 威爾遜的媽媽說：「有時候我真分不清他們兩個。」

phraseNotes.title: tell apart
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意為「區分」、「辨別」或「分辨兩者之間的差異」。

examples.english: The twins look so much alike that I can't tell them apart.
examples.chinese: 這對雙胞胎長得太像了，我實在分不出來。

sentences.englishOriginal: They really are two peas in a pod, said Chester's mother.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特的媽媽說：「他們兩個人真是如出一轍。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: two peas in a pod
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 英文經典成語，字面意思是「同一個豆莢裡的兩顆豆子」，用來形容兩個人性格、長相或行為極為相似，相當於中文的「如出一轍」或「形影不離」。

examples.english: They dress alike and share the same hobbies; they are like two peas in a pod.
examples.chinese: 他們穿著相似且愛好相同，簡直是如出一轍。

sentences.englishOriginal: And then Lilly moved into the neighborhood.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後，莉莉搬進了這個社區。

vocabularyNotes.title: neighborhood
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「鄰近地區」或「社區」。

examples.english: There is a beautiful park in our neighborhood.
examples.chinese: 我們社區裡有一個漂亮的公園。

sentences.englishOriginal: And she never left the house without one of her nifty disguises.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而且她每次出門一定會換上她那些俏皮的偽裝道具。

vocabularyNotes.title: nifty
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，非正式用語，形容事物「極好的」、「漂亮的」或「精巧俏皮的」。

examples.english: That is a nifty little gadget you have there.
examples.chinese: 你那個小玩意兒還真精巧。

vocabularyNotes.title: disguise
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「偽裝」或「喬裝打扮的服飾」。

examples.english: He wore a fake beard as a disguise.
examples.chinese: 他戴著假鬍鬚作為偽裝。

sentences.englishOriginal: Just when they were about to give up hope, a fierce-looking cat with horrible fangs jumped out of the bushes and frightened the older boys away.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 正當他們快要絕望的時候，一隻長著可怕尖牙、面目兇惡的貓從灌木叢裡跳了出來，把那些大男孩嚇跑了。

vocabularyNotes.title: fangs
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞（複數），指動物（如貓、狗、蛇等）的「尖牙」或「毒牙」。

examples.english: The dog bared its sharp fangs at the stranger.
examples.chinese: 那隻狗對著陌生人露出了銳利的尖牙。

examples.english: We never gave up hope even during the hardest times.
examples.chinese: 即使在最艱難的時刻，我們也從未放棄希望。

examples.english: The loud noise frightened the birds away.
examples.chinese: 巨大的聲響把鳥兒們都嚇跑了。

grammarNotes.title: fierce-looking (compound adjective)
grammarNotes.explanation: 複合形容詞，由「形容詞/副詞 + 現在分詞」組成，用來描述外觀「看起來很兇狠的」。

examples.english: A strange, sweet-smelling flower grew in the yard.
examples.chinese: 院子裡長出一朵奇特、聞起來很香的花。

sentences.englishOriginal: Chester and Wilson and Lilly, Lilly and Wilson and Chester. That's the way it was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 切斯特、威爾遜和莉莉，莉莉、威爾遜和切斯特。事情就是這樣。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: That's the way it was.
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在故事中用來作結的道地句子，表示「這就是當時的狀況」或「一向來都是如此」，有一種溫馨而肯定的故事節奏感。

examples.english: We worked hard every winter, and that's the way it was.
examples.chinese: 我們每個冬天都努力工作，情況向來就是如此。
